chunks.text: Alan J. Smith is a Professor Emeritus at EECS, UC Berkeley. Contact information includes 192 Soda Hall, tel: 510-642-5290, email: smith@eecs.berkeley.edu, and office hours by appointment only. Links provided are Personal Homepage, Publications, Dissertations, and Research Support, with Rachel Zhang as a contact at zhany21@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Alan J. Smith's position and contact details?

chunks.text: Alan J. Smith received his S.B. from MIT and a Ph.D. from Stanford University. Awards include the Harry Goode Award of the IEEE Computer Society in 2006 for contributions to cache and memory performance, the IEEE Reynold B. Johnson Information Storage Systems Award in 2008 for storage system performance analysis, and the A. A. Michelson Award in 2003 for lifetime achievements in computer measurement.
chunks.question: What are some key awards Alan J. Smith has received?
chunks.answer: Harry Goode Award and IEEE Reynold B. Johnson Award.

chunks.text: Alan J. Smith is a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (2001), ACM Fellow (2000), and IEEE Fellow (1988). His research areas include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC) and Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), focusing on computer system performance analysis, I/O systems, cache memories, and memory systems.
chunks.question: What fellowships and research areas are associated with Alan J. Smith?
chunks.answer: AAAS, ACM, IEEE Fellow; computer performance analysis.

chunks.text: Selected publications by Alan J. Smith include 'The automatic improvement of locality in storage systems' (2005), 'PACE: A new approach to dynamic voltage scaling' (2004), and 'The performance effect of I/O optimizations and disk improvements' (2004). Other works cover topics like multimedia instruction sets, database workloads, processor energy use, and cache consistency protocols.
chunks.question: What are some notable publications by Alan J. Smith?
chunks.answer: Works on storage systems and dynamic voltage scaling.

chunks.text: Additional selected publications include 'Analysis of benchmark characteristics and benchmark performance prediction' (1996), 'Performance characterization of optimizing compilers' (1995), and 'Disk cache -- Miss ratio analysis and design considerations' (1985). Earlier works involve cache memories, file migration, and multiprocessor interference, spanning from 1976 to 1990.
chunks.question: What topics do Alan J. Smith's earlier publications cover?
chunks.answer: Benchmark analysis, cache design, and file migration.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards for Alan J. Smith include American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) Fellow (2008), IEEE Reynold B. Johnson Information Storage Systems Award (2008), CS Harry H. Goode Memorial Award (2006), A. A. Michelson Award (2003), ACM Fellow (2001), and IEEE Fellow (1989).
chunks.question: What are the listed honors and awards for Alan J. Smith?
chunks.answer: AAAS Fellow, IEEE Johnson Award, Goode Award.
